# Integration test for cmd2mcpserver
#
# This test creates a simple program, converts it with cmd2mcpserver,
# and verifies the MCP server operates correctly

# Create test program with flags
-- testprog/main.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
)

var name = flag.String("name", "world", "name to greet")
var count = flag.Int("count", 1, "number of times to greet")
var verbose = flag.Bool("verbose", false, "enable verbose output")

func main() {
	flag.Parse()
	if *verbose {
		fmt.Printf("Greeting %s %d times\n", *name, *count)
	}
	for i := 0; i < *count; i++ {
		fmt.Printf("Hello, %s!\n", *name)
	}
}

-- testprog/go.mod --
module github.com/test/testprog

go 1.21

# Build cmd2mcpserver (assumes it's in the PATH or relative location)
# For testing, we use the built binary from the Go test environment
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver testprog
stdout "Server generated successfully"
stdout "mcpserver"

# Check generated files
exec ls mcpserver
stdout "main.go"
stdout "go.mod"
stdout "README.md"

# Verify the README contains usage information
exec cat mcpserver/README.md
stdout "testprog MCP Server"
stdout "Model Context Protocol"
stdout "go run main.go"

# Use dry-run to verify tool schema generation
exec go run ../../cmd/cmd2mcpserver -dry-run testprog
stdout "-- main.go --"
stdout "InputSchema.*json.RawMessage"
stdout '"properties"'
stdout '"name"'
stdout '"count"'
stdout '"verbose"'

# Verify multiple flags are properly detected
stdout '"type":\s*"string".*name'
stdout '"type":\s*"integer".*count'  
stdout '"type":\s*"boolean".*verbose'

# Verify proper flag descriptions are included
stdout 'name to greet'
stdout 'number of times to greet'
stdout 'enable verbose output'
